edits_summary.revealNode: Added a single line invoking this._playRevealHighlight(x, y) directly before this._playRevealAnimation(objs). No timing changes; both calls fire in the same tick from the same dispatch site that the pacing agent already sequenced.
edits_summary._playRevealAnimation: Added a brief amber stroke flash on the existing node circle. Captures circle.strokeColor before mutation so the restore exactly matches whichever color _spawnNodeObjects originally chose (0xffffff for unlocked, 0x888888 for locked). Reverted via this.time.delayedCall after 450 ms.
edits_summary._playRevealHighlight: New helper method (added inside the existing class — no new module/class/system). Spawns two short-lived Phaser Circle GameObjects: an inner glow burst and an outer expansion ring. Both destroy themselves in onComplete.

visual_elements_added: Outer ring expansion: stroked-only Phaser Circle, color 0xFFD27A, radius 22 -> tween scale to 80/22 (~3.64x), alpha 1.0 -> 0.0, duration 700 ms, ease Sine.easeOut. Implemented as scale tween on a Circle GameObject (Phaser Graphics radius cannot be tweened directly).
Inner glow burst: filled Phaser Circle, color 0xFFD27A, radius 40, alpha 0.4 -> 0.0, scale 1.0 -> 1.4, duration 500 ms, ease Sine.easeOut.
Stroke color flash on the existing node circle: 0xffffff/0x888888 -> 0xFFD27A -> original, duration 450 ms via setStrokeStyle + delayedCall (Phaser does not natively tween Arc stroke colors).
notes.stroke_flash_applied: Yes — applied. Captured circle.strokeColor at flash time (defaults to 0xffffff if numeric read fails) so we restore exactly to the color that _spawnNodeObjects set, which correctly preserves the gray locked-stroke (0x888888) for locked nodes. Stroke width 3 matches _spawnNodeObjects pattern.

depth_choices.rationale: Sits above landmarks (-25) so the ring/glow are visible against the map, and below the node container (depth 1) so the node icon, label, and stroke flash render crisp on top. Never obscures the node icon.

notes.no_leaks_confirmed: Both transient GameObjects (ring, glow) destroy themselves in tween onComplete callbacks. The stroke flash mutates an existing object (the node circle) and restores via delayedCall guarded by `if (circle && circle.scene)` so it no-ops if the scene tore down mid-animation. No persistent references held; no Graphics created.

scope_compliance.no_new_systems: All edits inside existing methods or new helper on the existing class. No new modules, no new classes, no new module-scope constants — every magic number is a named local.
scope_compliance.no_progression_changes: Untouched: revealArea, saveGame, redrawFog, _emitRegionDiscovered, first-discovery dialog, SFX dispatch.
scope_compliance.no_timing_changes: Did not alter delays, durations, or sequencing in revealNode itself. Sibling-1 (pacing) territory preserved.
scope_compliance.no_camera_changes: No camera tweens, no zoom, no pan. Sibling-3 territory preserved.
scope_compliance.hmr_preserved: registerSceneHmr line at file end unchanged.

manual_verification_scenarios: 1. Trigger first-ever discovery (fresh save, no prior tiles): observe amber ring expand from the node outward to ~80 px while glow burst haloes the node, simultaneously the node circle stroke briefly flashes amber then returns to white. Ring + glow disappear cleanly within ~700 ms; node remains at default scale 1.

2. Trigger reveal of a locked node (e.g., a node whose unlockReq is not yet met): confirm the stroke flash returns to the gray (0x888888) locked color, NOT white. The lock icon (🔒) stays crisp throughout — depth 0.5 keeps the glow underneath.

3. Reveal multiple nodes in rapid succession (drained pending queue at 700 ms + 380 ms stagger from the pacing agent): each node gets its own independent ring/glow that staggers naturally. Open Phaser scene inspector / DevTools and confirm no Arc/Circle GameObjects accumulate in the scene's display list after ~1 s post-reveal — every transient effect is destroyed.
notes.build_status: npm run build PASSED — vite build completed in 12.29s, 867 modules transformed, no errors. Existing chunk-size warnings (vendor-rapier, vendor-phaser, GamePage) are pre-existing and unrelated.
alternatives_considered: Skipping the stroke flash (suggested fallback) — rejected because reading circle.strokeColor on Phaser Arc is straightforward and the flash adds meaningful punch on the node itself, not just around it.
Tweening Graphics radius — rejected per the embedded prompt's guidance; scale tween on a Circle GameObject is visually equivalent and far cheaper.
